2605.20022.title: FlexDraft: Flexible Speculative Decoding via Attention Tuning and Bonus-Guided Calibration
2605.20022.paper_summary: Speculative decoding accelerates memory-bound LLM inference without quality degradation by using a fast drafter to propose multiple candidate tokens and the target model to verify them in parallel. However, conventional sequential speculative decoding suffers from mutual waiting between drafting and verification, and repeated exchange of intermediate states further increases memory access overhead. Parallel speculative decoding addresses this limitation by performing drafting and verification within a single target forward pass, allowing future drafts to be prepared while current candidates are being verified. Although effective at small batch sizes, existing parallel speculative decoding methods either require costly continual pretraining with quality degradation or suffer from low acceptance rates. More importantly, this paradigm inherently suffers from uncertainty in both the bonus token and the accepted length, leading to draft verification mismatch and causing throughput gains to collapse at large batch sizes. To address these limitations, we introduce FlexDraft, a lossless speculative decoding framework that flexibly adapts to varying batch sizes through three key designs. (1) Attention Tuning enables block diffusion drafting by tuning only the attention projectors of the final few layers on mask tokens, while keeping the autoregressive path frozen to preserve the target distribution and produce high quality drafts with minimal trainable parameters. (2) Bonus-guided Calibration uses a lightweight MLP conditioned on the resolved bonus token to calibrate draft logits, mitigating draft verification mismatch caused by bonus token uncertainty. (3) Flex Decoding dynamically switches between parallel draft and verify at small batch sizes and sequential draft then verify at large batch sizes, and adjusts verification length based on draft confidence to eliminate redundant computation.
2605.20022.paper_summary_zh: 推測解碼通過使用快速草擬器提出多個候選標記，並讓目標模型並行驗證它們，從而加速了記憶體受限的 LLM 推理而不降低質量。然而，傳統的順序推測解碼在草擬和驗證之間存在相互等待的問題，並且中間狀態的重複交換進一步增加了記憶體訪問的開銷。並行推測解碼通過在單次目標前向傳播中進行草擬和驗證來解決這一限制，允許在當前候選標記被驗證的同時準備未來的草擬。儘管在小批量大小下有效，但現有的並行推測解碼方法要麼需要昂貴的持續預訓練並伴隨質量下降，要麼面臨低接受率。更重要的是，這一範式本質上在獎勵標記和接受長度上都存在不確定性，導致草擬驗證不匹配，並使得在大批量大小下的吞吐量增益崩潰。為了解決這些限制，我們引入了 FlexDraft，一個無損的推測解碼框架，通過三個關鍵設計靈活適應不同的批量大小。(1) 注意力調整通過僅調整最後幾層在掩碼標記上的注意力投影器來實現區塊擴散草擬，同時保持自回歸路徑凍結，以保留目標分佈並以最小的可訓練參數生成高質量的草擬。(2) 獎勵引導校準使用一個輕量級的 MLP，根據已解決的獎勵標記來校準草擬邏輯，減輕由於獎勵標記不確定性引起的草擬驗證不匹配。(3) Flex Decoding 在小批量大小下動態切換並行草擬和驗證，在大批量大小下則順序草擬然後驗證，並根據草擬信心調整驗證長度，以消除冗餘計算。
2605.20022.author: Yaojie Zhang et.al.
2605.20022.authors: Yaojie Zhang, Jianuo Huang, Junlong Ke, Yuhang Han, Yongji Long, Tianchen Zhao, Biqing Qi, Linfeng Zhang
